channel.title: briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesBreakingOffshootNL, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Iranians are leaving the country just to access the internet
item.description: Iran has cut off its access to the global internet. To find an internet connection, some Iranians are traveling across the border with Turkey — even j...

item.title: Anthropic’s New Mythos A.I. Model Sets Off Global Alarms
item.description: Mythos has triggered emergency responses from central banks and intelligence agencies globally, as Anthropic decides who has access to the powerful mo...

item.title: Celeste Rivas Hernandez Died of ‘Multiple Penetrating Injuries,’ Medical Examiner Says
item.description: The Los Angeles police sought to keep the findings secret for months as they investigated the singer D4vd, who was charged this week with the murder o...

item.title: 2 Are Dead After ‘Chemical Emergency’ at a West Virginia Plant
item.description: As many as 21 others were treated at a hospital and at the scene after a “chemical release” of hydrogen sulfide at a plant in Nitro, W. Va., the autho...

item.title: Inside Linda McMahon's effort to dismantle the Department of Education
item.description: A former pro-wrestling executive, McMahon is now the education secretary Trump tasked with abolishing the agency. New Yorker writer Zach Helfand expla...

item.title: Gene therapy for a rare type of deafness shows lasting results
item.description: Researchers say a gene therapy allowed deaf children and adults as old as 32 to hear for the first time. The benefits have persisted for more than two...

item.title: The tariff refund process has begun for businesses. What about customers?
item.description: While shipping companies are pledging refunds for customers who directly paid tariff fees, the situation is much trickier for retailers.

item.title: Family influencers make the lifestyle look good. But kids pay the price, new book says
item.description: What does it mean to monetize your offspring? To turn their childhood into content? In Like, Follow, Subscribe Fortesa Latifi explores what drives par...

item.title: Wildfires in Georgia Destroy Homes and Set Off Evacuations
item.description: The fires are also causing poor air quality, expected to last into at least Thursday, for a corner of the Southeast.

item.title: Man Accused of Killing 3 in Spree Near Atlanta Dies in Jail
item.description: Olaolukitan Adon Abel, a 26-year-old Navy veteran, had been charged with killing a man and two women in a series of attacks across suburban Atlanta.

item.title: To Iran, Trump Blinked First by Extending the Cease-Fire
item.description: Iran’s leaders believe that they can withstand an enduring standoff longer than President Trump. The strategy could be economically devastating for av...

item.title: On Independence Day, Israel Honors Rabbi Who Called to ‘Flatten’ Gaza
item.description: Avraham Zarbiv became known on Israel’s right for demolishing buildings in Gaza. He lit a torch at a national ceremony on Tuesday evening.

item.title: Anthropic’s New Mythos A.I. Model Sets Off Global Alarms
item.description: Mythos has triggered emergency responses from central banks and intelligence agencies globally, as Anthropic decides who has access to the powerful mo...

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Reported Seizure of Ships Injects New Uncertainty Over Peace Talks
item.description: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards said they had seized two ships near the Strait of Hormuz. A Trump spokeswoman said the president did not view those action...

item.title: Pope Leo, Visiting African Prison, Urges Inmates to Find Hope Amid Despair
item.description: “Life is not defined solely by one’s mistakes,” the pope told inmates being held in a port city of Equatorial Guinea.
